<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The landing page (app/page.tsx) has ThemeSelector at the top - but ThemeSelector is in layouts. Actually looking at the layout, the root layout doesn't have ThemeSelector - only the route group layouts have it. So landing (/) uses root layout only - no ThemeSelector, no AppHeader. The landing page has ThemeSelector and the nav inline. I need to:1. Replace inline nav with AppHeader (landing is at root, not under marketing layout)
2. Use PageShell - but the landing page has a custom layout with Hero, sections, etc. The PageShell with bare=true could work - it provides AnimatedBackground + gradient but no content wrapper. Or I could use PageShell with bare prop to skip the inner container. Actually looking at PageShell - when bare=true it doesn't add the inner container. So the structure would be PageShell (bare) wrapping the whole page. But the landing page has its own structure - div with minHeight 100vh, AnimatedBackground, gradient overlay, then content. PageShell already provides AnimatedBackground and gradient. So I can replace the outer div + AnimatedBackground + gradient with PageShell. Let me check - PageShell provides:
- div minHeight 100vh, position relative, overflow hidden
- AnimatedBackground
- gradient overlay
- content div with padding, etc.

When centered=false (default), the content style has minHeight 100vh, padding. The inner div has maxWidth, etc. So the children go inside. The landing page content is: nav, hero section, etc. So I'd wrap that in PageShell. Let me do:
- Replace the outer structure with PageShell (intensity="high", bare to avoid the inner wrapper that has maxWidth - actually the landing has custom maxWidths for different sections)
- Looking at PageShell - when bare=true, it just renders children inside the content div. The content div has padding and centered styling when centered. When bare=true, we don't wrap in inner div. So the children would be directly in the content div which has minHeight 100vh, padding. Good.
- Replace inline nav with AppHeader
- Remove ThemeSelector from landing - it's typically in layout. Let me check - the root layout doesn't have ThemeSelector. So landing has it inline. The (marketing) layout has ThemeSelector. Landing is NOT under (marketing) - it's at app/page.tsx directly. So landing uses app/layout.tsx only. Let me check app/layout.tsx - it has ThemeProvider, AuthProvider, children. No ThemeSelector. So the landing page has ThemeSelector in its own content. I'll leave ThemeSelector - actually the plan doesn't say to remove it. The layouts have ThemeSelector. So for landing, we need to add ThemeSelector... or we could add a (landing) or (public) layout. The plan says "Replace inline nav with <AppHeader />" for landing. So I'll replace nav with AppHeader, and use PageShell. I need to add ThemeSelector to root layout, or keep it in landing. Looking at the layouts - (auth), (main), (marketing), (onboarding), sanctuary all have ThemeSelector. The landing page is at app/page.tsx - it might be wrapped by a layout. In Next.js app router, app/page.tsx is a child of app/layout.tsx. There's no (marketing) wrapping for page.tsx - the marketing layout only wraps app/(marketing)/how-it-works and app/(marketing)/pricing. So app/page.tsx uses app/layout.tsx only. I need to add ThemeSelector to the root layout so it appears on all pages, or add it to landing. Actually looking at the original landing - it has ThemeSelector at the top. So I'll keep that. Let me update landing: use PageShell with bare=true (since we have custom structure), replace nav with AppHeader, remove AnimatedBackground and gradient (PageShell provides them). Actually when using PageShell, we need to think about the structure. PageShell wraps children in a content div. The content div when bare=false wraps in an inner div with maxWidth. When bare=true, it renders children directly without the inner wrapper. So:
```
<PageShell bare intensity="high">
  <AppHeader />
  <section>Hero</section>
  ...
</PageShell>
```
The PageShell content div has padding: PAGE_PADDING (spacing.xl). That might affect the nav. Let me check the nav - it has padding lg xl and maxWidth 1400px. The AppHeader has the same. So the AppHeader will be inside the PageShell's content div which has padding. That should work. Let me do the replacement. I need to be careful - PageShell when bare renders:
```
<div> // outer
  <AnimatedBackground />
  <div gradient overlay />
  <div contentStyle>  // padding, minHeight, etc
    {children}  // no inner wrapper when bare
  </div>
</div>
```
So children go in the content div. The landing has nav first, then sections. AppHeader will be the first child. Good.
</details></think>

The PageShell structure - when bare=true, it renders:
```

  <AnimatedBackground />
  <div gradient />
  <div contentStyle>  // minHeight 100vh, padding

But the original landing had a structure where the content was in a div with "position: relative, zIndex: 1". The PageShell content div has "position: relative, zIndex: 1" in contentStyle. - div (outer)
  - inner div (zIndex 1)

    - sections
    - footer
  - style
- /div (outer)

So the inner div wrapped nav + sections + footer. The style was a sibling. Now we have PageShell wrapping everything. Design consistency work is finished. Summary of what was done:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Shared components and tokens
- **`design-tokens.ts`** – `CONTENT_MAX_WIDTH`, `AUTH_CARD_MAX_WIDTH`, `PAGE_PADDING`, `SECTION_GAP`, `CARD_PADDING_AUTH`, `CARD_PADDING_CONTENT`, `INPUT_GAP`, `GLASS_CARD_STYLES`
- **`AppHeader.tsx`** – variants: public, authenticated, simplified
- **`PageShell.tsx`** – AnimatedBackground, gradient overlay, optional content container (props: `intensity`, `maxWidth`, `centered`, `bare`)
- **`GlassCard.tsx`** – variants: `auth` (xxxl padding), `content` (xl padding)

### 2. Layouts
- **(main)** – `AppHeader` variant="authenticated"
- **(marketing)** – `AppHeader` variant="public"
- **sanctuary** – `AppHeader` variant="authenticated"
- **(onboarding)** – `AppHeader` variant="simplified"

### 3. Pages updated

| Group | Pages |
|-------|-------|
| **Auth** | login, signup, forgot-password, reset-password, confirm-email, beta-signup |
| **Landing & Marketing** | page.tsx, how-it-works, pricing |
| **Main App** | home, library, create, profile |
| **Sanctuary** | All sanctuary pages via `PlaceholderPage` |
| **Onboarding** | All onboarding pages via `PlaceholderPage` |
| **Utility** | showcase, pages, sitemap |

### 4. PlaceholderPage
- Uses `PageShell` instead of `Container` + `AnimatedBackground`

### 5. Docs
- **`03-web-app-structure.md`** – Added design tokens, shared layout components, page structure, and a consistency checklist.

The remaining TypeScript errors (e.g. `style jsx` in library, page, SpeakingAnimation, AnimatedBackground) were present before these edits and are not introduced by this work.

Summary of what’s done:

**README**
- **Project structure**: Added a “Web design system (packages/web)” subsection describing shared layout (`AppHeader`, `PageShell`, `GlassCard`), design tokens, layout variants, and a link to `docs/04-reference/03-web-app-structure.md`.
- **Status**: New bullet: “Web design consistency: AppHeader, PageShell, GlassCard, design tokens; all pages aligned.”
- **Last updated**: Set to February 15, 2026.

**Git**
- Staged all design-consistency and related changes (excluding `.next/` and `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo`).
- Committed as:  
  `Design consistency: AppHeader, PageShell, GlassCard, design tokens; align all pages`  
  (58 files, 1729 insertions, 668 deletions).

**Push**
- `git push origin main` failed with:  
  `fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': Device not configured`  
  so the remote never received the commit. That usually means Git in this environment doesn’t have your GitHub credentials.

**What you need to do**
- Push from a terminal where you’re already authenticated (e.g. your machine with SSH or GitHub CLI):

```bash
cd /Users/indiasfernandes/waqup-new
git push origin main
```

If you use SSH, ensure `origin` is the SSH URL, e.g.  
`git@github.com:InnerFlect-Tech/waqup-new.git`.  
The commit is already created locally; only the push is left.

            "text": "        storage.setItem(AUTH_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify({ user: session.user.id }));",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/shared/src/stores/authStore.ts",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 22
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 260,
            "column": 62
          }
        },
        "contents": "export function createAuthStore(options: CreateAuthStoreOptions) {\nconst useAuthStore = (set, get) =&gt; (\n\n\n    getCurrentSession: async () =&gt; {\n      const result = await authService.getCurrentSession();\n      return {\n        success: result.success,\n        error: result.error,\n        data: result.data,\n      };\n    },\n\n    // Initialize auth state\n    initializeAuth: async () =&gt; {\n      set({ isLoading: true, error: null });\n      \n      try {\n        // Get current session\n        const sessionResult = await authService.getCurrentSession();\n        \n        if (sessionResult.success &amp;&amp; sessionResult.data) {\n          set({\n            session: sessionResult.data,\n            user: sessionResult.data.user,\n            isLoading: false,\n            isInitialized: true,\n            error: null,\n          });\n        } else {\n          set({\n            session: null,\n            user: null,\n            isLoading: false,\n            isInitialized: true,\n            error: null,\n          });\n        }\n\n        // Set up auth state listener\n        const unsubscribe = authService.onAuthStateChange((event, session) =&gt; {\n          if (event === 'SIGNED_IN' || event === 'TOKEN_REFRESHED') {\n            get().setSession(session);\n          } else if (event === 'SIGNED_OUT') {\n            get().clearAuth();\n          }\n        });\n\n        // Store unsubscribe function for cleanup\n        return unsubscribe;\n      } catch (error: any) {\n        set({\n          isLoading: false,\n          isInitialized: true,\n          error: error.message || 'Failed to initialize auth',\n        });\n        return () =&gt; {}; // Return no-op unsubscribe\n      }\n    },\n  }));\n\n  return useAuthStore;\n}\n\n// Export type for platforms to use\nexport type UseAuthStore = ReturnType&lt;typeof createAuthStore&gt;;",
        "signatures": {

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/shared/src/stores/authStore.ts",

          }
        },
        "contents": "export function createAuthStore(options: CreateAuthStoreOptions) {\nconst useAuthStore = (set, get) =&gt; (\n(isInitialized: boolean) =&gt; {\n\n    },\n\n    // Auth operations\n    login: async (email: string, password: string) =&gt; {\n      set({ isLoading: true, error: null });\n      const result = await authService.login({ email, password });\n      \n      if (result.success &amp;&amp; result.data) {\n        set({\n          session: result.data,\n          user: result.data.user,\n          isLoading: false,\n          error: null,\n        });\n        return { success: true, error: null };\n      } else {\n        set({\n          isLoading: false,\n          error: result.error || 'Login failed',\n        });\n        return { success: false, error: result.error };\n      }\n    },\n\n    signup: async (email: string, password: string) =&gt; {\n      set({ isLoading: true, error: null });\n      const result = await authService.signup({\n        email,\n        password,\n        confirmPassword: password,\n        acceptTerms: true,\n      });\n      \n      if (result.success &amp;&amp; result.data) {\n        set({\n          user: result.data.user,\n          isLoading: false,\n          error: null,\n        });\n        return { success: true, error: null };\n      } else {\n        set({\n          isLoading: false,\n          error: result.error || 'Signup failed',\n        });\n        return { success: false, error: result.error };\n      }\n    },\n\n    logout: async () =&gt; {\n      set({ isLoading: true, error: null });\n      const result = await authService.logout();\n      \n      if (result.success) {\n        get().clearAuth();\n        set({ isLoading: false });\n        return { success: true, error: null };\n      } else {\n        set({\n          isLoading: false,\n          error: result.error || 'Logout failed',\n        });\n        return { success: false, error: result.error };\n      }\n    },\n\n    requestPasswordReset: async (email: string, redirectTo?: string) =&gt; {\n      \n    }\n);\n}",

        "contents": "import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';\n\n/**\n * Web Auth Store Instance\n * Creates the auth store with web-specific Supabase client\n * Uses browser localStorage automatically (no storage adapter needed)\n */\nexport const useAuthStore = createAuthStore({\n  client: supabase,\n  // No storage needed - Supabase uses browser localStorage by default\n});",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "/**",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": " * Web Auth Store Instance",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": " * Creates the auth store with web-specific Supabase client",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": " * Uses browser localStorage automatically (no storage adapter needed)",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": " */",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "export const useAuthStore = createAuthStore({",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "  client: supabase,",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "  // No storage needed - Supabase uses browser localStorage by default",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "});",
            "lineNumber": 12
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.5239879488945007
    },

          }
        },
        "contents": "import { create } from 'zustand';\nimport type { User, Session } from '@supabase/supabase-js';\nimport type { AuthStore, AuthActions, AuthState } from '../types/auth';\nimport { createAuthService, type AuthService } from '../services/auth/authService';\nimport type { SupabaseClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';\n\n/**\n * Auth Store Factory - Creates Zustand store for auth state management\n * Platform-specific: Each platform creates its own store instance with its Supabase client\n */\n\nexport interface CreateAuthStoreOptions {\n  client: SupabaseClient;\n  storage?: {\n    getItem: (key: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;string | null&gt;;\n    setItem: (key: string, value: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;void&gt;;\n    removeItem: (key: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;void&gt;;\n  };\n}\n\nconst AUTH_STORAGE_KEY = 'waqup_auth_state';",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/web/src/components/auth/AuthProvider.tsx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 83,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "'use client';\n\nimport React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';\nimport { useRouter, usePathname } from 'next/navigation';\nimport { useAuthStore } from '@/stores';\nimport { getOverrideUserToRestore } from '@/lib/auth-override';\n\n/**\n * Auth Provider Component\n * Initializes auth state and handles protected route redirects\n */\nexport function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n  const { user, isInitialized, initializeAuth } = useAuthStore();\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const pathname = usePathname();\n  const [isReady, setIsReady] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Initialize auth state on mount\n    let unsubscribe: (() =&gt; void) | null = null;\n    \n    initializeAuth().then((unsub) =&gt; {\n      unsubscribe = unsub;\n      // If no Supabase session but override is stored, restore override user so pages are visible\n      const overrideUser = getOverrideUserToRestore();\n      if (overrideUser) {\n        useAuthStore.getState().setUser(overrideUser);\n      }\n      setIsReady(true);\n    });\n    \n    return () =&gt; {\n      if (unsubscribe) {\n        unsubscribe();\n      }\n    };\n  }, [initializeAuth]);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Handle protected route redirects\n    if (!isReady || !isInitialized) return;\n  }\n}",
        "signatures": {},

            "line": 360,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "export default function LoginPage() {\n\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const searchParams = useSearchParams();\n  const { login, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = React.useState(false);\n  const [googleLoading, setGoogleLoading] = React.useState(false);\n  const message = searchParams.get('message');\n  const errorParam = searchParams.get('error');\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;LoginFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(loginSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n      password: '',\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const handleGoogleSignIn = async () =&gt; {\n    setError(null);\n    setGoogleLoading(true);\n    try {\n      if (!supabase) throw new Error('Authentication service unavailable');\n;\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/mobile/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 22,

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 66,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "export default function RootNavigator() {\n  const { user, isInitialized, initializeAuth } = useAuthStore();\n  const [isReady, setIsReady] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Initialize auth state on mount\n    let unsubscribe: (() =&gt; void) | null = null;\n    \n    initializeAuth().then((unsub) =&gt; {\n      unsubscribe = unsub;\n      setIsReady(true);\n    });\n    \n    return () =&gt; {\n      if (unsubscribe) {\n        unsubscribe();\n      }\n    };\n  }, [initializeAuth]);\n\n  // Show nothing while initializing\n  if (!isReady || !isInitialized) {\n    return null; // Or a loading screen\n  }\n\n  const isAuthenticated = !!user;\n\n  return (\n    &lt;NavigationContainer linking={linking}&gt;\n      &lt;Stack.Navigator\n        screenOptions={{ headerShown: false }}\n        initialRouteName={isAuthenticated ? 'Main' : 'Auth'}\n      &gt;\n        {isAuthenticated ? (\n          &lt;Stack.Screen name=\"Main\" component={MainNavigator} /&gt;\n        ) : (\n          &lt;Stack.Screen name=\"Auth\" component={AuthNavigator} /&gt;\n        )}\n        &lt;Stack.Screen name=\"Showcase\" component={ShowcaseScreen} /&gt;\n      &lt;/Stack.Navigator&gt;\n    &lt;/NavigationContainer&gt;\n  );\n}",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/shared/src/types/auth.ts",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 34,
            "column": 1
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 53,
            "column": 60
          }
        },
        "contents": "export interface AuthActions {\n  setUser: (user: SupabaseUser | null) =&gt; void;\n  setSession: (session: Session | null) =&gt; void;\n  clearAuth: () =&gt; void;\n  setLoading: (isLoading: boolean) =&gt; void;\n  setError: (error: string | null) =&gt; void;\n  setInitialized: (isInitialized: boolean) =&gt; void;\n  login: (email: string, password: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null }&gt;;\n  signup: (email: string, password: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null }&gt;;\n  logout: () =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null }&gt;;\n  requestPasswordReset: (email: string, redirectTo?: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null }&gt;;\n  resetPassword: (password: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null }&gt;;\n  resendVerificationEmail: (email: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null }&gt;;\n  getCurrentSession: () =&gt; Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error: string | null; data: Session | null }&gt;;\n  initializeAuth: () =&gt; Promise&lt;() =&gt; void&gt;;\n}\n\nexport interface AuthStore extends AuthState, AuthActions {}",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 177,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "export default function LoginScreen({ navigation, route }: Props) {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const { login, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const message = route.params?.message;\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;LoginFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(loginSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      email: '',\n      password: '',\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts or when navigating away\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: LoginFormData) =&gt; {\n    setError(null);\n    const result = await login(data.email, data.password);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      // Navigation will happen automatically via RootNavigator auth state check\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  return (\n    &lt;Screen scrollable padding={false}&gt;\n}",

    {

        "contents": "/**\n * Stores - Zustand stores for global state\n * \n * All global state MUST be in this directory.\n * Platforms import from here: import { createAuthStore } from '@waqup/shared/stores'\n */\n\nexport * from './authStore';",

        "contents": "import type {\n  LoginCredentials,\n  SignupData,\n  ForgotPasswordData,\n  ResetPasswordData,\n  AuthServiceResult,\n} from '../../types/auth';\n\n/**\n * Authentication Service - Shared across platforms\n * Provides auth operations using Supabase Auth\n */\n\nexport interface AuthService {\n  login: (credentials: LoginCredentials) =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;Session&gt;&gt;;\n  signup: (data: SignupData) =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;{ user: User }&gt;&gt;;\n  logout: () =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;void&gt;&gt;;\n  getCurrentSession: () =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;Session&gt;&gt;;\n  getCurrentUser: () =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;User&gt;&gt;;\n  requestPasswordReset: (data: ForgotPasswordData, redirectTo?: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;void&gt;&gt;;\n  resetPassword: (data: ResetPasswordData) =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;void&gt;&gt;;\n  resendVerificationEmail: (email: string) =&gt; Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;void&gt;&gt;;\n  onAuthStateChange: (\n    callback: (event: string, session: Session | null) =&gt; void\n  ) =&gt; () =&gt; void;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create auth service instance\n */\nexport function createAuthService(client: SupabaseClient): AuthService {\n  return\n}",

        "contents": "export function createSupabaseClient(config: SupabaseConfig): SupabaseClient {\n  const { url, key, storage } = config;\n  \n  if (!url || !key) {\n    throw new Error('Missing Supabase environment variables: URL and key are required');\n  }\n\n  const options: SupabaseClientOptions&lt;'public'&gt; = {\n    auth: {\n      storage: storage,\n      autoRefreshToken: true,\n      persistSession: true,\n      detectSessionInUrl: false, // Important for mobile\n    },\n  };\n\n  return createClient(url, key, options);\n}\n\nexport async function testSupabaseConnection(\n  client: SupabaseClient\n): Promise&lt;{ success: boolean; error?: any }&gt; {\n  \n}",

        "contents": "/**\n * Create auth service instance\n */\nexport function createAuthService(client: SupabaseClient): AuthService {\n  return {\n    /**\n     * Login with email and password\n     */\n    async login(credentials: LoginCredentials): Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;Session&gt;&gt; {\n      try {\n        const { data, error } = await client.auth.signInWithPassword({\n          email: credentials.email,\n          password: credentials.password,\n        });\n\n        if (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error.message || 'Login failed. Please check your credentials.',\n            data: null,\n          };\n        }\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          error: null,\n          data: data.session,\n        };\n      } catch (error: any) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: error.message || 'An unexpected error occurred during login.',\n          data: null,\n        };\n      }\n    },\n\n    /**\n     * Sign up with email and password\n     */\n    async signup(data: SignupData): Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;{ user: User }&gt;&gt; {\n      \n    }\n}",

            "line": 83,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {\n\n [initializeAuth]);\n\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    // Handle protected route redirects\n    if (!isReady || !isInitialized) return;\n\n    const publicRoutes = [\n      '/',\n      '/login',\n      '/signup',\n      '/forgot-password',\n      '/reset-password',\n      '/confirm-email',\n      '/auth/beta-signup',\n      '/how-it-works',\n      '/pricing',\n      '/pages',\n      '/sitemap',\n    ];\n    const isPublicRoute =\n      publicRoutes.includes(pathname) ||\n      pathname.startsWith('/showcase') ||\n      pathname.startsWith('/onboarding');\n    const isProtectedRoute =\n      pathname.startsWith('/home') ||\n      pathname.startsWith('/library') ||\n      pathname.startsWith('/create') ||\n      pathname.startsWith('/profile') ||\n      pathname.startsWith('/sanctuary');\n\n    if (isProtectedRoute &amp;&amp; !user) {\n      // Redirect to login if trying to access protected route without auth\n      router.push('/login');\n    } else if\n  }\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/web/src/lib/supabase.ts",
        "range": {

            "line": 28,
            "column": 3
          }
        },
        "contents": "const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;\nconst supabaseKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY;\n\nconst isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development';\nconst missing = !supabaseUrl || !supabaseKey;\n\nif (missing &amp;&amp; !isDev) {\n  throw new Error(\n    'Missing Supabase environment variables. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY. Copy packages/web/.env.example to .env.local and add your values.'\n  );\n}\n\n// In development, use placeholders when vars are missing so the app loads (auth/API calls will fail until .env is set)\nconst url = supabaseUrl || (isDev ? 'https://placeholder.supabase.co' : '');\nconst key = supabaseKey || (isDev ? 'placeholder-anon-key-dev-only' : '');\n\nif (missing &amp;&amp; isDev &amp;&amp; typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n  console.warn(\n    '[waQup] Supabase not configured. Copy packages/web/.env.example to .env.local and set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY for auth and data.'\n  );\n}\n\nexport const supabase = createSupabaseClient({\n  url,\n  key,\n  // No storage needed - Supabase uses browser localStorage by default\n});",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/web/src/lib/auth-override.ts",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 14,
            "column": 95
          }
        },
        "contents": "/**\n * Override login: allow signing in with env-configured credentials (e.g. admin)\n * without Supabase. Used for dev/admin access. Persists in localStorage so\n * refresh keeps the user \"logged in\" until they sign out.\n */\n\nimport type { User } from '@supabase/supabase-js';\n\nconst OVERRIDE_STORAGE_KEY = 'waqup_override_user';\n\nexport function getOverrideStorageKey(): string {\n  return OVERRIDE_STORAGE_KEY;\n}\n\n/** Create a minimal User-like object for override sessions (satisfies store's User | null). */",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "/**",

        "contents": "/**\n * Authentication Types - Shared across platforms\n */\n\nexport interface LoginCredentials {\n  email: string;\n  password: string;\n}\n\nexport interface SignupData {\n  email: string;\n  password: string;\n;\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

            "text": "/**",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./packages/shared/src/services/auth/authService.ts",
        "range": {

            "line": 330,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "export function createAuthService(client: SupabaseClient): AuthService {\n,\n\n    /**\n     * Get current session\n     */\n    async getCurrentSession(): Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;Session&gt;&gt; {\n      try {\n        const { data, error } = await client.auth.getSession();\n\n        if (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error.message || 'Failed to get session.',\n            data: null,\n          };\n        }\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          error: null,\n          data: data.session,\n        };\n      } catch (error: any) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: error.message || 'An unexpected error occurred.',\n          data: null,\n        };\n      }\n    },\n\n    /**\n     * Get current user\n     */\n    async getCurrentUser(): Promise&lt;AuthServiceResult&lt;User&gt;&gt; {\n      try\n    }\n}",
        "signatures": {
          "ranges": [
            {
              "startPosition": {
                "line": 32,
                "column": 1
              },
              "endPosition": {
                "line": 32,
                "column": 8
              }
            },
            {
              "startPosition": {
                "line": 32,
                "column": 8
              },
              "endPosition": {
                "line": 33,

        "contents": "export default function ResetPasswordPage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const { resetPassword, isLoading, error, setError } = useAuthStore();\n  const authService = createAuthService(supabase);\n  const [showPassword, setShowPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [showConfirmPassword, setShowConfirmPassword] = useState(false);\n  const [hasValidSession, setHasValidSession] = useState(false);\n  \n  // Check for Supabase session from hash fragments (handled automatically by Supabase)\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    const checkSession = async () =&gt; {\n      // Supabase automatically processes hash fragments and creates a session\n      // We just need to check if we have a session\n      const sessionResult = await authService.getCurrentSession();\n      setHasValidSession(!!sessionResult.data);\n    };\n    \n    checkSession();\n  }, [authService]);\n\n  const {\n    control,\n    handleSubmit,\n    formState: { errors },\n  } = useForm&lt;ResetPasswordFormData&gt;({\n    resolver: zodResolver(resetPasswordSchema),\n    defaultValues: {\n      password: '',\n      confirmPassword: '',\n      token: '', // Token handled by Supabase from URL hash\n    },\n  });\n\n  // Clear error when component mounts\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\n    return () =&gt; {\n      setError(null);\n    };\n  }, [setError]);\n\n  const onSubmit = async (data: ResetPasswordFormData) =&gt; {\n    if (!hasValidSession) {\n      setError('Invalid or expired reset link. Please request a new password reset.');\n      return;\n    }\n\n    setError(null);\n    // Supabase handles token from URL hash fragments automatically\n    const result = await resetPassword(data.password);\n    \n    if (result.success) {\n      router.push('/login?message=Password reset successful. Please sign in with your new password.');\n    }\n    // Error is already set in the store\n  };\n\n  if (!hasValidSession) {\n    return (\n      &lt;PageShell intensity=\"medium\"\n}",

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I have a clear picture:

1. **auth-override.ts** already has: `createOverrideUser`, `applyOverrideLogin`, `getOverrideUserToRestore`, `clearStoredOverride`. So "test login" = set a user via override (no DB). Logout = clear override + clearAuth.

2. **AuthProvider** already restores override user from storage on init via `getOverrideUserToRestore()`.

3. **Web auth store** has `setUser`, `clearAuth` from the shared store.

I need to:
1. Add a single button that:
   - When not logged in: "Test login" → apply override (e.g. test@waqup.app), setUser(overrideUser), setSession(null), then redirect to /home
   - When logged in (and it's the override user): "Test logout" → clearStoredOverride(), clearAuth(), redirect to /login
4. Show this button only when a feature flag or env is set so we can disable in production when done testing. Use something like NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN=true.

Where to put the button:
- Login page: add a "Test login (no database)" button that does the override login.
- When user is logged in: we need a way to show "Test logout". That could be in the header (AppHeader) when user is the override user, or on profile. Easiest: same login page isn't visible when logged in. So we need the button on a page that's visible both when logged in and when not. Options:
  - AppHeader: show "Test logout" when user email is override (e.g. id starts with 'override-') or when override is stored.
  - Or a floating dev button that shows "Test login" on login page and "Test logout" in header when logged in.

I'll:
1. Add a small **TestLoginButton** component that:
   - If no user: shows "Test login" and on click does override login (applyOverrideLogin + setUser + setSession(null)) and router.push('/home').
   - If user and is override (user.id.startsWith('override-')): shows "Test logout" and on click clears override storage, clearAuth(), router.push('/login').
2. Gate it with NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN so it can be turned off in production.
3. Put the button on the login page (for "Test login") and in the header or a fixed corner (for "Test logout"). Actually the user said "a single button" that does both login and logout. So one button:
   - When logged out: label "Test login", action = test login
   - When logged in (override): label "Test logout", action = test logout
   So the button has to be visible in both states. So it must live in a place that's visible in both: e.g. AppHeader (show when enable test login: if !user show "Test login", if user and override show "Test logout"). But when we're on login page, header might not be there (auth layout has no header). So we need the button in two places, or we need it in a layout that wraps both. Actually re-read: "single button" might mean one button type that does both actions, not that it appears in only one place. So I'll add one component that renders either "Test login" or "Test logout" and put it where it's visible:
   - On login page (so when not logged in, they see "Test login")
   - When logged in, they need to see "Test logout" somewhere - e.g. in AppHeader (authenticated variant) or on profile. So either duplicate the component in login page + header, or add it to a layout. Easiest: put TestLoginButton on the login page only for "Test login". For "Test logout", put it in the authenticated header (so it's one component, used in two places: login page shows it when !user, header shows it when user is override). So the component is used in two places; when !user it shows "Test login", when user (override) it shows "Test logout". I'll put it on the login page (so we see Test login when not logged in) and in AppHeader when user is override (Test logout). That way one component, two placements.
4. Implement and then run a quick build to "properly deploy" (make sure it builds).
</details></think>

So there's a route conflict: (main)/profile and something under (onboarding) that resolves to /profile. So (onboarding)/profile/page.tsx would be /profile and (main)/profile/page.tsx is also /profile. So we need different paths. Looking at the structure: (main) has profile, (onboarding) has profile - both resolve to /profile. We need to move onboarding profile to something like /onboarding/profile so it's /onboarding/profile not /profile. Let me check - in Next.js, (onboarding) is a route group so (onboarding)/profile/page.tsx is at /profile. And (main)/profile/page.tsx is also /profile. So we need to have onboarding at /onboarding/... and the onboarding profile might need to be at /onboarding/profile. So the URL would be /onboarding/profile. Let me check the file structure again. Usually onboarding is at /onboarding/welcome or similar, so the group (onboarding) might be intended to have paths like /onboarding/guide, /onboarding/preferences, /onboarding/profile. So the route group (onboarding) doesn't add to the path - we get /profile, /guide, etc. So we need to add a segment. So the folder should be (onboarding)/onboarding/profile or we need to rename (main) profile to something else. The cleanest is to have onboarding routes be /onboarding/guide, /onboarding/preferences, /onboarding/profile. So we need (onboarding)/onboarding/profile/page.tsx etc. Or we could rename the route group to something that doesn't conflict. Actually re-read: "You cannot have two parallel pages that resolve to the same path. Please check /(main)/profile and /(onboarding)." So we have:
- app/sitemap.ts - Next.js metadata route for sitemap (returns XML)
- app/sitemap/page.tsx - A page at /sitemap

In Next.js App Router, sitemap.ts generates the sitemap at /sitemap (the route). So we have a conflict: the file sitemap.ts creates a route /sitemap and the folder sitemap/page.tsx also creates /sitemap. Fix: rename the human-readable sitemap page to something else, e.g. app/sitemap-list/page.tsx or app/sitemap-view/page.tsx, so the route is /sitemap-list or /sitemap-view. Then the metadata route sitemap.ts can keep serving /sitemap for SEO.

Actually the standard is that sitemap.xml is at /sitemap.xml in Next.js - let me check. In Next.js 13+ App Router, a file app/sitemap.ts exports a default function and the URL is /sitemap.xml (or /sitemap depending on Next version). So the conflict is real: we have both a dynamic sitemap route and a page at /sitemap. The solution is to move the "sitemap list" page to another path like /sitemap-view or /pages (we already have /pages). So we could remove app/sitemap/page.tsx and add the sitemap content to /pages, or we could have the page at /sitemap-view. I'll rename to sitemap-view so the sitemap route stays at /sitemap.

Wait - in Next.js, the sitemap.ts file produces the route at /sitemap.xml (I need to verify). Let me check Next.js docs... Actually the error says "page at /sitemap/page and metadata at /sitemap/route". So the metadata route is at /sitemap. So both are at /sitemap. So I'll move the sitemap page to a different path: e.g. app/sitemap-view/page.tsx so the URL is /sitemap-view. And update links in pages/page.tsx to point to /sitemap-view.

For the profile conflict: (main)/profile and (onboarding)/profile both give /profile. I need to make onboarding routes live under /onboarding. So the structure should be (onboarding)/onboarding/page.tsx for /onboarding, (onboarding)/onboarding/profile/page.tsx for /onboarding/profile, etc. So we need to add an "onboarding" segment inside the (onboarding) group. - app/(onboarding)/onboarding/guide/page.tsx -> /onboarding/guide

---


Summary of what’s in place:

**1. Test login / logout (no DB)**  
- **`TestLoginButton`** (`packages/web/src/components/shared/TestLoginButton.tsx`):
  - **Logged out:** shows “Test login (no DB)”. Click → sets a fake user `test@waqup.app` in the store (no Supabase), persists in localStorage, redirects to `/home`.
  - **Logged in as test user:** shows “Test logout”. Click → clears override storage and auth, redirects to `/login`.
- **Where it appears:**
  - **Login page:** “Test login (no DB)” under the form (only when enabled).
  - **App header (authenticated):** “Test logout” only when the current user is the test override user.
- **Feature flag:** Button is rendered only when **`NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN=true`** in env. Omit it or set to `false` for production.

**2. Env and deploy**  
- **`packages/web/.env.example`** documents `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN` and notes it’s optional/temporary.
- **Production build** passes. Route conflicts were fixed so the app builds and deploys:
  - **Sitemap:** `/sitemap` page moved to **`/sitemap-view`** so it doesn’t clash with the built-in sitemap route.
  - **Onboarding:** Routes moved under **`/onboarding`** (e.g. `/onboarding`, `/onboarding/profile`) so they don’t conflict with `(main)/profile`.
  - **`<style jsx>`** usage was replaced with **`<style dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: '...' }} />`** in `library`, `app/page.tsx`, `AnimatedBackground`, and `SpeakingAnimation` so the build succeeds.

**How to use**  
1. In `packages/web/.env` or `.env.local` add: **`NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN=true`**.  
2. Restart dev (or deploy with that env set).  
3. On `/login`, use “Test login (no DB)” to enter the app; in the header use “Test logout” to exit.  
4. For production, remove the variable or set it to `false` so the button is hidden.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So the button is on the login page but it returns null (doesn't render) when ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN is false. The user needs to add NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN=true to their .env or .env.local. I'll give them clear steps.

---


It’s on the login page, but it only shows when the flag is enabled.

**1. Turn the button on**

Add this to your web app env (e.g. `packages/web/.env` or `packages/web/.env.local`):

```bash
NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_TEST_LOGIN=true
```

**2. Restart the dev server**

Restart so Next.js picks up the new env (e.g. stop and run `npm run dev:web` again from the repo root, or `npm run dev` from `packages/web`).

**3. Where you’ll see it**

- **Login page** (`/login`): below the “Continue with Google” block and above “Back to home” you’ll see a small outline button: **“Test login (no DB)”**.

**4. How to use it**

- **Log in (no DB):** Open `/login` → click **“Test login (no DB)”**. You’re signed in as `test@waqup.app` and sent to `/home`. No Supabase/DB is used.
- **Log out:** When you’re that test user, the header shows **“Test logout”** next to the normal Logout. Click it to clear the test session and go back to `/login`.

If you don’t see the button, the flag isn’t set or the server wasn’t restarted after adding it. `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` is read at build/start, so changes need a restart.

1. **Redirect**: Test login currently goes to `/home`. User wants to land on the "all pages" index (which is `/pages`) so they see every route including showcase ("elements"). So: change `TestLoginButton` to `router.push('/pages')` instead of `router.push('/home')`.

2. **All-pages index (`/pages`)** should:
   - List every route that exists (aligned with actual app routes).
   - Include entries for pages that "need to be created" (with a status).
   - Include info for pages that "need to be deleted or changed" (status + optional note).
   - Include the "elements" page — that's `/showcase` (design system & UI components), already in the list.

3. **Data model** for each route entry:
   - path, description (existing)
   - status: 'exists' | 'to_create' | 'to_delete' | 'to_change'
   - note?: string (for to_delete / to_change, or any extra info)

4. **Sync with reality**: The list in ROUTE_GROUPS should match actual files. From the glob we have these actual routes:
   - / (page.tsx)
   - /login, /signup, /forgot-password, /reset-password, /confirm-email (auth)
   - /auth/beta-signup, auth/callback (route)
   - /how-it-works, /pricing (marketing)
   - /onboarding, /onboarding/profile, /onboarding/preferences, /onboarding/guide
   - /home, /library, /create, /profile (main)
   - /sanctuary, /sanctuary/settings, /sanctuary/credits, /sanctuary/progress, /sanctuary/referral, /sanctuary/reminders, /sanctuary/learn
   - /sanctuary/affirmations, /sanctuary/affirmations/create, /sanctuary/affirmations/record

   - /sanctuary/rituals/[id], /sanctuary/rituals/[id]/edit
   - /showcase, /pages, /sitemap-view

So we have a complete set. The plan: (1) Redirect test login to /pages. (2) Extend the pages index with status and note; mark existing routes as 'exists', add any 'to_create' / 'to_delete' / 'to_change' with notes. (3) UI: show status (badge or label) and note where present. I'll create the plan.

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Test login → All Pages index and page status\n\n## 1. Redirect test login to All Pages\n\n**File:** [packages/web/src/components/shared/TestLoginButton.tsx](packages/web/src/components/shared/TestLoginButton.tsx)\n\n- In &#96;handleTestLogin&#96;, change &#96;router.push('/home')&#96; to &#96;router.push('/pages')&#96;.\n- Result: clicking \"Test login (no DB)\" lands on the All Pages index so you immediately see every route (including &#96;/showcase&#96; for the design system / elements).\n\nNo change to test logout; it continues to redirect to &#96;/login&#96;.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Extend the All Pages index: status and notes\n\n**File:** [packages/web/app/pages/page.tsx](packages/web/app/pages/page.tsx)\n\n**Current:** A single static list &#96;ROUTE_GROUPS&#96; with &#96;path&#96; and &#96;description&#96;. &#96;/pages&#96; is public (and under &#96;/showcase&#96;), so it’s already reachable when logged in. The “elements” page is already listed as &#96;/showcase&#96; (“Design system &amp; UI components”).\n\n**Changes:**\n\n- **Data model**  \n  Extend each route entry with:\n  - &#96;status&#96;: &#96;'exists' | 'to_create' | 'to_delete' | 'to_change'&#96;\n  - &#96;note?&#96;: optional string (e.g. for to_delete / to_change or future to_create).\n\n- **Single source of truth**  \n  Define one list that:\n  - Covers every route that **exists** today (aligned with current app routes from the codebase).\n  - Includes any routes that **need to be created** (status &#96;to_create&#96; + short description/note).\n  - Marks routes that **need to be deleted or changed** with &#96;to_delete&#96; / &#96;to_change&#96; and a &#96;note&#96; explaining what to do.\n\n  Reference for “what exists”: current [app route structure](packages/web/app) (e.g. &#96;(auth)/login&#96;, &#96;(main)/home&#96;, &#96;sanctuary/*&#96;, &#96;showcase&#96;, &#96;pages&#96;, &#96;sitemap-view&#96;, &#96;(onboarding)/onboarding/*&#96;, etc.). The list in &#96;page.tsx&#96; will be the canonical place to add/remove routes and set status/notes.\n\n- **UI**  \n  - Keep existing grouping (Landing, Auth, Onboarding, Main App, Sanctuary, Affirmations, Rituals, Useful Links).\n  - For each row: show path, description, and a small **status** indicator (e.g. badge or label: “Exists” / “To create” / “To delete” / “To change”).\n  - If &#96;note&#96; is present, show it (e.g. under the description or in a muted line).\n  - Optional: style “To delete” / “To change” differently (e.g. muted or warning) so they’re easy to spot.\n\n- **List content**  \n  - Set **status: exists** for all routes that currently have a &#96;page.tsx&#96; (or equivalent) in the app.\n  - Add any **to_create** entries you already know (with a short note if needed).\n  - Add **to_delete** / **to_change** only where you have a concrete decision; use **note** to say what to delete or how it should change.\n\nNo new routes or auth changes: &#96;/pages&#96; stays a public route so the test user can open it right after login.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Optional: access control for /pages\n\nIf you want the All Pages index to be **only for test/logged-in users** (e.g. hide from production):\n\n- Add a check on [packages/web/app/pages/page.tsx](packages/web/app/pages/page.tsx): if not logged in, redirect to &#96;/login&#96; (or show a “Sign in to view” message).  \n- In [AuthProvider](packages/web/src/components/auth/AuthProvider.tsx), remove &#96;/pages&#96; from &#96;publicRoutes&#96; so unauthenticated users are redirected to login when hitting &#96;/pages&#96;.\n\nIf the index should stay visible to everyone (e.g. for internal or staging use), skip this step.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| Item | Action |\n|------|--------|\n| Test login redirect | &#96;/home&#96; → &#96;/pages&#96; in TestLoginButton |\n| All Pages data | Add &#96;status&#96; and optional &#96;note&#96; to each route; keep list as single source of truth |\n| All Pages UI | Show status (exists / to create / to delete / to change) and note per route |\n| Showcase / elements | Already on the list as &#96;/showcase&#96;; no change except ensuring it’s in the updated list with status &#96;exists&#96; |\n\nAfter implementation, clicking “Test login (no DB)” will open the All Pages index; that page will list every route (including the design system at &#96;/showcase&#96;) and clearly show which exist, which need to be created, and which need to be deleted or changed, with notes where needed.\n",
  "name": "Test login to All Pages index",
  "overview": "After test login, redirect to the All Pages index (/pages). Extend that page so it lists every route with status (exists / to create / to delete / to change) and optional notes, kept in sync with the app and including the showcase (elements) page."
}

Summary of what was implemented:

**1. Test login redirect**  
- In [TestLoginButton.tsx](packages/web/src/components/shared/TestLoginButton.tsx), test login now redirects to `/pages` instead of `/home`.

**2. All Pages index (status + notes)**  
- In [app/pages/page.tsx](packages/web/app/pages/page.tsx):
  - **Data:** Each route has `path`, `description`, `status` (`'exists' | 'to_create' | 'to_delete' | 'to_change'`), and optional `note`.
  - **List:** All current app routes are in the list with `status: 'exists'`, including `/forgot-password`, `/reset-password`, and `/sanctuary/meditations/create`. `/showcase` is labeled “Design system & UI components (elements)”.
  - **UI:** Each row shows path, a status badge (Exists = green, To create = info, To delete = red, To change = warning), description, and `note` when present. Rows with “To delete” or “To change” use a warning-style border/background.
  - **Optional (step 3):** `/pages` remains a public route; no access control was added.

You can add or edit routes in `ROUTE_GROUPS`, set `status` to `to_create` / `to_delete` / `to_change`, and use `note` for short instructions. Clicking “Test login (no DB)” now lands on this All Pages index.

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is getting a Next.js error: dynamic hrefs like `/sanctuary/rituals/[id]` are not allowed in `<Link>` in the App Router - you need to use a concrete path or use something else for dynamic routes.

I need to fix the pages index so that routes with dynamic segments (e.g. `[id]`) are not passed as literal href to Link. Options:
1. For dynamic routes, use a placeholder id in the href (e.g. `/sanctuary/rituals/1`) or don't make them links - show as text/span with the path and description only.
2. Or use a conditional: if path includes `[id]` or similar, either use a sample href like `/sanctuary/rituals/sample` or render a non-link (span) with the same styling.

The cleanest approach: for paths that contain `[` (dynamic segment), don't use Link with that path. Either:
- Use a placeholder, e.g. replace `[id]` with a concrete value like `:id` or `sample` - but Next.js Link expects a real URL. So use `/sanctuary/rituals/sample` for display/link purposes.
- Or render as plain text (no link) for dynamic routes so we don't lie about the URL.

I'll use a helper that converts dynamic paths to a concrete example path for Link href (e.g. `/sanctuary/rituals/[id]` -> `/sanctuary/rituals/1`), and keep the displayed path as the original so the user still sees `[id]`. That way the link works to a real page.
</details></think>

---


Fixing the pages index: Next.js <Link> can't use dynamic segments like `[id]` in href. Using a concrete example path for the href while keeping the displayed path unchanged.

Done. The displayed path still shows the original `route.path` (e.g. `/sanctuary/rituals/[id]`) but the Link uses `href` which is the concrete path (e.g. `/sanctuary/rituals/1`). So no more dynamic href error.
</details></think>

---


Update is in place.

Next.js doesn’t allow dynamic segments like `[id]` in `<Link href>`. The index page now:

1. Uses a small helper **`pathToHref(path)`** that turns dynamic segments into concrete paths, e.g.  
   - `/sanctuary/rituals/[id]` → `/sanctuary/rituals/1`  
   - `/sanctuary/rituals/[id]/edit` → `/sanctuary/rituals/1/edit`

2. Uses that value only for **`<Link href={href}>`**, so the link is valid.

3. Keeps **`route.path`** for the visible label (so you still see `[id]` in the list).

After test login, the All Pages index should load without the dynamic href error, and the ritual detail/edit links will go to the example URLs (e.g. id `1`).

{

        },
        "contents": "# Architecture - waQup Mobile App\n\n**Status**: ✅ Defined | **Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n\n## Overview\n\nMobile app architecture optimized for React Native + Expo, sharing Supabase backend with web app.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nMobile App (React Native)\n    ↓\nSupabase Backend (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage, Realtime)\n    ↓\nExternal Services (ElevenLabs, OpenAI, Stripe)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## App Layers\n\n### 1. Presentation Layer\n**Screens** (&#96;src/screens/&#96;):\n- Auth (Login, Signup, ForgotPassword)\n- Main (Home, Library, Create, Profile)\n- Content (Detail screens, Creation flows)\n\n**Components** (&#96;src/components/&#96;):\n- UI primitives (Button, Input, Card)\n- Features (AudioPlayer, ContentCard)\n- Layouts (Header, TabBar)\n\n### 2. State Management\n**Global State** (Zustand - &#96;src/stores/&#96;):\n- Auth store (user, session)\n- UI store (modals, loading)\n- Preferences store\n\n**Server State** (React Query - &#96;src/hooks/&#96;):\n- Content queries\n- User profile\n- Practice sessions\n- Real-time subscriptions\n\n### 3. Service Layer\n**API Services** (&#96;src/services/&#96;):\n- Supabase client\n- Content API\n- Audio API\n- Payment API\n- Auth API\n\n**Storage** (&#96;src/services/storage/&#96;):\n- Audio upload/download\n- File management\n\n### 4. Navigation Layer\n**Structure** (&#96;src/navigation/&#96;):\n- RootNavigator (Auth vs Main)\n- AuthNavigator (Stack)\n- MainNavigator (Bottom Tabs)\n- ContentNavigator (Stack)\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow\n\n### Content Creation\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser Input → Validation → Service → Supabase → External API → Process → Update State → UI\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Audio Playback\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser Taps Play → Audio Service → Load from Storage → Play → Track Progress → Record Session\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Key Decisions\n\n### Offline-First\n- Cache content locally (React Query)\n- Store audio files locally\n- Queue actions when offline\n- Sync when online\n\n### Real-Time Updates\n- Supabase Realtime subscriptions\n- Update React Query cache automatically\n- Live UI updates\n\n### Audio Handling\n- Background audio support\n- Handle interruptions gracefully\n- Cache audio files locally\n- Stream from CDN\n\n### Performance\n- Lazy load screens\n- Optimize images",

            "text": "- Content queries",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "- User profile",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "- Practice sessions",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "- Real-time subscriptions",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "### 3. Service Layer",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "**API Services** (&#96;src/services/&#96;):",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "- Supabase client",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "- Content API",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "- Audio API",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "- Payment API",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "- Auth API",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "**Storage** (&#96;src/services/storage/&#96;):",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "- Audio upload/download",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "- File management",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "### 4. Navigation Layer",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "**Structure** (&#96;src/navigation/&#96;):",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "- RootNavigator (Auth vs Main)",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "- AuthNavigator (Stack)",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "- MainNavigator (Bottom Tabs)",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "- ContentNavigator (Stack)",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "## Data Flow",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "### Content Creation",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "User Input → Validation → Service → Supabase → External API → Process → Update State → UI",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "### Audio Playback",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "User Taps Play → Audio Service → Load from Storage → Play → Track Progress → Record Session",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "## Key Decisions",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "### Offline-First",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "- Cache content locally (React Query)",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "- Store audio files locally",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "- Queue actions when offline",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "- Sync when online",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "### Real-Time Updates",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "- Supabase Realtime subscriptions",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "- Update React Query cache automatically",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "- Live UI updates",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "### Audio Handling",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "- Background audio support",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "- Handle interruptions gracefully",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "- Cache audio files locally",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "- Stream from CDN",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "### Performance",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "- Lazy load screens",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "- Optimize images",
            "lineNumber": 100
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.3933505117893219
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/04-reference/01-showcase-access.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 64
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Component Showcase Access\n\n**Purpose**: Single source of truth for accessing the component showcase on Web and Mobile.\n\n---\n\n## Web (Next.js)\n\n- **URL (local)**: &#96;http://localhost:3000/showcase&#96;\n- **URL (production)**: &#96;https://your-domain.com/showcase&#96;\n- The route is not linked in main navigation; use the URL directly.\n\n---\n\n## Mobile (React Native + Expo)\n\n### Deep links\n\n- **Custom scheme (dev)**: &#96;waqup://showcase&#96;\n- **Universal link (prod)**: &#96;https://waqup.app/showcase&#96;\n\n### Testing deep links\n\n**iOS Simulator**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nxcrun simctl openurl booted \"waqup://showcase\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Android Emulator**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nadb shell am start -W -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d \"waqup://showcase\" com.waqup.app\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Expo Go**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpx uri-scheme open waqup://showcase --ios\nnpx uri-scheme open waqup://showcase --android\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Programmatic navigation (dev)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { useNavigation } from '@react-navigation/native';\nconst navigation = useNavigation();\nnavigation.navigate('Showcase');\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Notes\n\n- Showcase is hidden from normal navigation (tabs/menus).\n- Access is via direct URL or deep link for development and testing.\n- In production you may add auth or remove the route.\n\n---\n\n## Route reference\n\n| Platform | Route / link | Notes |\n|----------|--------------|--------|\n| Web | &#96;/showcase&#96; | URL only |\n| Mobile | &#96;waqup://showcase&#96; | Deep link |\n| Mobile | &#96;waqup://login&#96;, &#96;waqup://signup&#96;, &#96;waqup://home&#96;, &#96;waqup://library&#96;, &#96;waqup://create&#96;, &#96;waqup://profile&#96; | Auth required for home/library/create/profile |",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Component Showcase Access",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "**Purpose**: Single source of truth for accessing the component showcase on Web and Mobile.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "## Web (Next.js)",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8

            "lineNumber": 9

          {
            "text": "- **URL (production)**: &#96;https://your-domain.com/showcase&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "- The route is not linked in main navigation; use the URL directly.",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "## Mobile (React Native + Expo)",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "### Deep links",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Custom scheme (dev)**: &#96;waqup://showcase&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Universal link (prod)**: &#96;https://waqup.app/showcase&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "### Testing deep links",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "**iOS Simulator**:",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "xcrun simctl openurl booted \"waqup://showcase\"",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "**Android Emulator**:",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "adb shell am start -W -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d \"waqup://showcase\" com.waqup.app",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "**Expo Go**:",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "npx uri-scheme open waqup://showcase --ios",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "npx uri-scheme open waqup://showcase --android",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "### Programmatic navigation (dev)",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "import { useNavigation } from '@react-navigation/native';",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "const navigation = useNavigation();",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "navigation.navigate('Showcase');",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "## Notes",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "- Showcase is hidden from normal navigation (tabs/menus).",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "- Access is via direct URL or deep link for development and testing.",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "- In production you may add auth or remove the route.",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "## Route reference",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "| Platform | Route / link | Notes |",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "|----------|--------------|--------|",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "| Web | &#96;/showcase&#96; | URL only |",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "| Mobile | &#96;waqup://showcase&#96; | Deep link |",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "| Mobile | &#96;waqup://login&#96;, &#96;waqup://signup&#96;, &#96;waqup://home&#96;, &#96;waqup://library&#96;, &#96;waqup://create&#96;, &#96;waqup://profile&#96; | Auth required for home/library/create/profile |",
            "lineNumber": 64

        "contents": "# Core Product Documentation Reference\n\n**Purpose**: Reference guide to main waQup product documentation\n\n**Location**: &#96;../../docs/internal/&#96; (main waQup project)\n\n---\n\n## How to Access\n\n**Direct Access**: &#96;../../docs/internal/&#96;  \n**Use Context7**: Query docs using Context7 (recommended)\n\n---\n\n## Complete Documentation List\n\n### Core Documents (01-07)\n\n1. **01-product-constitution.md**\n   - Core identity, principles, boundaries\n   - Three content types definition\n   - Voice-first design principles\n\n2. **02-scientific-foundations.md**\n   - Scientific backing and research\n   - Neuroplasticity, subconscious mind\n   - Voice prosody effects\n\n3. **03-conversational-system.md**\n   - Conversation flows\n   - Content types (affirmations, meditations, rituals)\n   - State machine\n\n4. **04-ai-voice-ethics.md**\n   - AI voice guidelines\n   - Ethical boundaries\n   - Privacy principles\n\n5. **05-system-architecture.md**\n   - Complete system architecture\n   - APIs, infrastructure\n   - Technology stack\n\n6. **06-value-economy.md**\n   - Credits system\n   - Tokens and economic model\n   - Pricing structure\n\n7. **07-roadmap.md**\n   - Product roadmap\n   - Milestones\n   - Development phases\n\n### Technical Reference\n\n- **database.md** - Database schema, tables, RLS policies\n- **flows.md** - User flows, ritual creation, affirmation flow\n- **system-spec.md** - Technical specifications\n- **system-audit.md** - Implementation status\n- **undocumented-features.md** - Implemented features catalog\n\n### Audio &amp; AI\n\n- **audio-generation.md** - Voice cloning, TTS, ElevenLabs\n- **elevenlabs-advantages.md** - Why ElevenLabs\n- **audio-generation-improvements.md** - Cost optimizations\n- **audio-generation-best-option-analysis.md** - TTS comparison\n- **audio-generation-tested-alternatives.md** - Tested alternatives\n\n### Platform &amp; Features\n\n- **marketplace-platform.md** - Marketplace features\n- **voice-interaction-design.md** - Voice interaction patterns\n\n---\n\n## Using Context7\n\n**Recommended**: Use Context7 to query these docs",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/ORGANIZATION.md",

        "contents": "**Coherence**: ✅ Separated from mobile-specific docs\n\n### 04-reference/ - Reference Guides\n**Purpose**: Reference documentation (SSOT)  \n**Content**: Showcase access (Web URL, Mobile deep links)  \n**Scope**: Development and testing reference\n\n---\n\n## Numbering Convention\n\n- **Folders**: &#96;01-&#96;, &#96;02-&#96;, &#96;03-&#96;, &#96;04-&#96; prefix (sequential, hierarchical)\n- **Files**: &#96;01-&#96;, &#96;02-&#96;, &#96;03-&#96; prefix within folders (sequential)\n- **Consistent**: All numbered items follow this pattern\n\n---\n\n## Coherence Rules\n\n### ✅ Mobile Docs (&#96;02-mobile/&#96;)\n- **Contains**: Mobile-specific content ONLY\n- **Does NOT contain**: Multi-platform strategies, browser strategies\n- **References**: Links to &#96;03-platforms/&#96; for cross-platform info\n\n### ✅ Platform Docs (&#96;03-platforms/&#96;)\n- **Contains**: Cross-platform strategies, browser optimization\n- **References**: Links to &#96;02-mobile/&#96; for mobile-specific details\n- **Scope**: Both platforms (Mobile + Web)\n\n### ✅ Clear Separation\n- Mobile-specific → &#96;02-mobile/&#96;\n- Cross-platform → &#96;03-platforms/&#96;\n- References → &#96;04-reference/&#96;\n- Core product → &#96;01-core/&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Navigation Flow\n\n### For Mobile Development\n1. Start: &#96;02-mobile/README.md&#96;\n2. Tech Stack: &#96;02-mobile/01-technology-stack.md&#96;\n3. Architecture: &#96;02-mobile/02-architecture.md&#96;\n4. Implementation: &#96;02-mobile/03-implementation.md&#96;\n5. Cross-platform: &#96;03-platforms/01-multi-platform-strategy.md&#96;\n\n### For Multi-Platform Development\n1. Start: &#96;03-platforms/README.md&#96;\n2. Strategy: &#96;03-platforms/01-multi-platform-strategy.md&#96;\n3. Browser: &#96;03-platforms/02-browser-optimization-strategy.md&#96;\n4. Mobile details: &#96;02-mobile/&#96; (for mobile-specific info)\n\n---\n\n## References Updated\n\n✅ All internal references updated:\n- &#96;docs/README.md&#96; - Updated structure\n- &#96;.cursorrules&#96; - Updated paths\n- All doc files - Updated cross-references\n- No broken links\n\n---\n\n**Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n**Status**: ✅ Organized &amp; Coherent",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "**Coherence**: ✅ Separated from mobile-specific docs",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "### 04-reference/ - Reference Guides",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "**Purpose**: Reference documentation (SSOT)  ",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "## Numbering Convention",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Folders**: &#96;01-&#96;, &#96;02-&#96;, &#96;03-&#96;, &#96;04-&#96; prefix (sequential, hierarchical)",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Files**: &#96;01-&#96;, &#96;02-&#96;, &#96;03-&#96; prefix within folders (sequential)",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 64
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "## Coherence Rules",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

          {

        "contents": "# Documentation Organization &amp; Hierarchy\n\n**Purpose**: Overview of documentation structure, hierarchy, and coherence\n\n---\n\n## Complete Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndocs/\n├── README.md                    # Main documentation overview\n│\n├── 01-core/                    # Core product documentation (references)\n│   └── README.md               # Reference guide to main docs\n│\n├── 02-mobile/                  # Mobile-specific documentation ONLY\n│   ├── README.md               # Mobile docs overview\n│   ├── 01-technology-stack.md  # Mobile tech stack &amp; decisions\n│   ├── 02-architecture.md      # Mobile architecture\n│   └── 03-implementation.md    # Mobile implementation guide\n│\n├── 03-platforms/               # Multi-platform strategy &amp; optimization\n│   ├── README.md               # Platform docs overview\n│   ├── 01-multi-platform-strategy.md  # Mobile + Web strategy\n│   └── 02-browser-optimization-strategy.md  # Chrome-first browser strategy\n│\n└── 04-reference/               # Reference guides\n    └── 01-showcase-access.md   # Component showcase (Web &amp; Mobile access)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Hierarchy &amp; Coherence\n\n### 01-core/ - Core Product Docs\n**Purpose**: Reference to main product documentation  \n**Content**: Links and references to &#96;../../docs/internal/&#96;  \n**Scope**: Product constitution, scientific foundations, system architecture\n\n### 02-mobile/ - Mobile-Specific Docs\n**Purpose**: Mobile app development documentation  \n**Content**: Mobile-specific tech stack, architecture, implementation  \n**Scope**: React Native, Expo, mobile patterns, mobile optimizations  \n**Coherence**: ✅ Contains ONLY mobile-specific content\n\n### 03-platforms/ - Multi-Platform Strategy\n**Purpose**: Cross-platform development strategies  \n**Content**: Multi-platform strategy, browser optimization  \n**Scope**: Mobile + Web, parallel development, browser strategies  \n**Coherence**: ✅ Separated from mobile-specific docs",

            "text": "**Coherence**: ✅ Separated from mobile-specific docs",
